skill.name: subagent-driven-development
skill.description: Coordinate sequential coding subagents with SHA tracking, scoped implementation tasks, and two review gates for spec compliance and code quality.
skill.summary: Run multi-task development plans through isolated implementers and diff-based review cycles.
skill.author: CodingCossack

security_audit.summary: Static findings for weak cryptography and system reconnaissance are false positives from SHA tracking examples and review checklist text. The skill is not malicious, but it intentionally guides agents to run git commands, tests, commits, and possibly reviewer verification commands.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution in Development Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill instructs agents to run tests, lint, typecheck, commits, and git diffs. This is expected for a coding workflow, but it can execute project-controlled commands in the current repository.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files directly instruct agents to run development commands and git diffs. The commands are legitimate for the skill purpose, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Reviewer May Rerun Reported Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The quality reviewer template asks whether claimed test commands can be rerun. If an untrusted implementer report supplies unsafe commands, a reviewer could execute them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The template frames command reruns as optional and contextual. The risk depends on whether users paste or execute untrusted reported commands without inspection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SHA Tracking Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analysis flagged SHA-related text and nearby lines as weak cryptography. Manual review found commit SHA tracking examples, not hashing algorithms or security-sensitive crypto.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain workflow prose, task labels, and commit SHA references. No cryptographic operation or weak algorithm usage was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hidden Home Directory Example
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The hidden path is an example answer about user-level hook installation. It does not instruct hidden persistence, secret access, or unauthorized filesystem reads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line appears inside a fictional walkthrough and names a configuration directory. There is no command that creates, reads, or exfiltrates files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Terms
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: Static analysis flagged review checklist and example issue text. Manual review found quality-review language, not host reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content asks agents to check requirements, edge cases, and code quality. No uname, whoami, process listing, network discovery, or host inventory commands appear.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Execution Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes command execution as part of its intended coding workflow. Users should only run generated or reported commands after reviewing them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Command execution instructions are explicit and central to the workflow. The risk is bounded when used in trusted repositories with normal development safeguards.

dangerous_patterns.title: Git Diff Review Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Reviewer templates instruct agents to inspect diffs between supplied SHAs. This is appropriate but depends on trustworthy SHA values and repository context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The git commands are visible and limited to diff review. They still rely on repository state and user-supplied task context.

content.value_statement: Multi-task coding plans can lose scope, context, and review rigor as work progresses. This skill provides a sequential subagent workflow with SHA tracking and review gates.
actual_capabilities: Defines a controller workflow for sequential subagent execution.
Requires BASE_SHA and HEAD_SHA tracking for each task.
Provides implementer, spec reviewer, and code quality reviewer prompt templates.
Uses diff-based review to bound each task assessment.
Requires reported commands, test results, changed files, and scope confirmation.
Shows an example workflow with review cycles and final review.
limitations: It is a process guide, not an executable orchestration tool.
It assumes access to git, task tracking, and subagent dispatch.
It does not validate command safety before execution.
It depends on accurate task specifications and honest reports.

use_cases.title: Execute a planned feature safely
use_cases.description: Run a multi-step implementation plan with one focused subagent per task and review gates after each commit.

use_cases.title: Review scoped agent work
use_cases.description: Check whether each task changed only what the specification required before moving to code quality review.

use_cases.title: Coordinate AI coding sessions
use_cases.description: Maintain clear task state, SHA references, review cycles, and completion status during longer agent-assisted development.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Single Task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for one scoped implementation task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven development for this task. Capture BASE_SHA, send the full task text to one implementer, require tests, commit, and report HEAD_SHA.

prompt_templates.title: Run Spec Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow after the implementer reports completion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this task for spec compliance only. Compare the diff from BASE_SHA to HEAD_SHA against the task requirements and report file-line issues.

prompt_templates.title: Run Quality Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow after spec compliance passes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review code quality for the approved task diff. Check correctness, maintainability, test quality, and verification claims before approval.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate a Full Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for several dependent tasks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute the full plan sequentially with one implementer per task. Track BASE_SHA, HEAD_SHA, review cycles, status, and final cross-task review.

output_examples.input: Run task three from my implementation plan with review gates.
output_examples.output: The assistant records the current base commit, dispatches one implementer, waits for a commit report, then runs spec and quality reviews.

output_examples.input: The spec reviewer found a missing requirement.
output_examples.output: The assistant sends the issue back to the implementer, updates the task head commit after the fix, and repeats spec review.
The assistant runs a final review across the full implementation range and then proceeds to branch finishing steps.

best_practices: Use trusted repositories and inspect commands before running them.
Keep each task small enough for one clear commit or commit stack.
Require file-line review feedback before accepting task completion.
anti_patterns: Do not run several implementers in the same working tree.
Do not let reviewers assess the whole repository instead of the task diff.
Do not proceed when SHA tracking, test output, or changed files are missing.

faq.question: Does this skill write code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides prompts and process rules for coordinating coding subagents.

faq.question: Why does it require BASE_SHA and HEAD_SHA?
faq.answer: The SHA range lets reviewers inspect only the changes made for the current task.

faq.question: Can tasks run in parallel?
faq.answer: Only in separate branches or worktrees. The skill forbids parallel implementers in one working tree.

faq.question: What are the two review gates?
faq.answer: The first checks spec compliance. The second checks code quality after spec compliance passes.

faq.question: Is this safe for untrusted projects?
faq.answer: Use caution. The workflow asks agents to run development commands, so commands must be reviewed first.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
